concept_name: Coulomb's Law — Force Between Two Point Charges
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.1 Electric Charges and Fields (§1.5 Basic Properties of Electric Charge, §1.6 Coulomb's Law, §1.5a Forces between Multiple Charges / Principle of Superposition) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. HC Verma consulted for SEQUENCE (a force acts between charges without contact → its magnitude law F = k q₁q₂/r² → sign sets attract vs repel → the pair is equal and opposite → the 1/r² falloff → proportionality to the product → force is a vector → superposition of many charges), never copied. The atomic idea: two point charges exert an electric force on each other along the line joining them, equal and opposite (Newton's third law), of size F = k q₁q₂/r² — proportional to each charge and falling as the inverse square of the separation; with several charges the net force on one is the VECTOR SUM of the individual pair forces (superposition). This diamond is the VACUUM law only — the dielectric-medium factor F = F_vacuum/K is authored as a SEPARATE simulation per founder decision (2026-06-23). It is the foundation prerequisite of the electric_field_point_charge / force_on_charge_in_field / electric_field_dipole diamonds. New field_3d scenario coulombs_law_force. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a).

real_world_anchor.primary: Run a plastic comb through dry hair and hold it over tiny bits of paper — the paper jumps up to the comb. That snatch is the Coulomb force between the charge on the comb and the opposite charge it pulls to the surface of the paper. Bring the comb closer and the pull gets sharply stronger; rub it harder so it carries more charge and the pull grows too — exactly the two knobs of F = k q₁q₂/r².
real_world_anchor.secondary: Inside every atom an electron is held to the nucleus by nothing but this same Coulomb force, F = k e²/r². NCERT notes it is about 10³⁹ times stronger than the gravity between them — the law that lifts a scrap of paper is the law that holds matter together.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: In a monsoon thundercloud, charge slowly separates between the cloud base and the ground. The Coulomb force between them climbs as the charge grows until the air can no longer hold it back, and it discharges as a lightning strike.

formulas.coulomb_scalar: F = k q₁ q₂ / r²,  k = 9 × 10⁹ N·m²/C²
formulas.vector_form: F₁₂ = (k q₁ q₂ / r²) r̂  — directed along the line joining the charges
formulas.sign_rule: like charges repel, unlike attract;  F₁₂ = −F₂₁ (equal and opposite)
formulas.inverse_square: F ∝ 1/r²  — double r gives one quarter the force
formulas.product_rule: F ∝ q₁ q₂  — double either charge doubles the force
formulas.superposition: F_net = F₁ + F₂ + …  — the vector sum of the separate pair forces

constraints: The Coulomb force acts across empty space (action at a distance) — the charges need no contact, no wire and no medium to push or pull on each other
The two forces are ALWAYS equal in magnitude and opposite in direction (Newton's third law), even when one charge is far larger than the other — because the product q₁q₂ is the same for both
Like signs repel and unlike attract; reversing one charge's sign reverses BOTH arrows but leaves the magnitude k q₁q₂/r² unchanged
The force falls off as the inverse square of separation: doubling r drops it to one quarter, tripling r to one ninth — never merely to one half
The force is proportional to the product of the charges: doubling either charge doubles the force felt by BOTH charges equally
With several charges the net force on any one is the vector sum of its individual pair forces — each pair acts independently (principle of superposition)

aha_moment.statement: Double the distance and the force drops to a quarter, not a half.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Step the second charge out to 2r and 3r and watch the force arrow collapse to one quarter, then one ninth, of its length.

STATE_1.title: Two charges push on each other

tts_sentences.text_en: Here are two charges sitting a little apart, with nothing between them but empty space.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ दो charges थोड़ी दूरी पर बैठे हैं, और उनके बीच में कुछ भी नहीं है — बस empty space है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ రెండు charges కొంచెం దూరంగా ఉన్నాయి, వాటి మధ్య ఏమీ లేదు — కేవలం empty space మాత్రమే ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Each one feels a push from the other — a real force — even though nothing is touching.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों एक-दूसरे से एक push महसूस करते हैं — एक असली force — भले ही कुछ भी touch नहीं कर रहा।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి ఒక్కటి మరొక దాని నుండి ఒక push అనుభవిస్తుంది — నిజమైన force — అయినా ఏదీ touch అవడం లేదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two like charges, both positive, push each other apart, as the green arrows show.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो like charges, दोनों positive, एक-दूसरे को दूर push करते हैं, जैसा कि green arrows दिखा रहे हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు like charges, రెండూ positive, ఒకదానినొకటి దూరంగా push చేసుకుంటాయి, green arrows చూపినట్టు.

tts_sentences.text_en: This electric push across empty space is the Coulomb force. The whole lesson is about how big it is and which way it points.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Empty space में यह electric push Coulomb force कहलाती है। पूरा lesson इसी के बारे में है — यह कितनी बड़ी है और किस direction में point करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Empty space లో ఈ electric push ను Coulomb force అంటారు. మొత్తం lesson ఇది ఎంత పెద్దది మరియు ఏ direction లో point చేస్తుందో అని చెప్పడానికే.

misconception_watch.belief: Charges must touch, or need air or a wire between them, to push or pull on each other
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Show the force arrows spanning the empty gap between the two charges with nothing connecting them.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The Coulomb force acts straight across empty space — no contact and no medium are needed.

tts_sentences.text_en: Coulomb measured this force and found one clean rule for its size.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Coulomb ने इस force को measure किया और इसकी size के लिए एक सरल rule खोजा।
tts_sentences.text_te: Coulomb ఈ force ని measure చేసి దాని size కోసం ఒక సులభమైన rule కనుగొన్నాడు.

tts_sentences.text_en: The force equals k times the first charge times the second charge, divided by the distance squared.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Force equals k times the first charge times the second charge, divided by the distance squared।
tts_sentences.text_te: Force equals k times the first charge times the second charge, divided by the distance squared.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here r is the distance between the charges, and k is Coulomb's constant.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ r, charges के बीच की distance है, और k, Coulomb's constant है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ r అంటే charges మధ్య distance, మరియు k అంటే Coulomb's constant.

tts_sentences.text_en: Do not let Coulomb's constant k fool you — it is about nine times ten to the ninth, a huge number. That is why even tiny charges feel a real force.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Coulomb's constant k को आपको confuse मत होने दीजिए — यह nine times ten to the ninth के बराबर है, यह एक बहुत बड़ा number है। इसीलिए tiny charges भी एक real force महसूस करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Coulomb's constant k చూసి confuse అవ్వకండి — ఇది nine times ten to the ninth, చాలా పెద్ద number. అందుకే tiny charges కూడా ఒక real force అనుభవిస్తాయి.

misconception_watch.belief: k is a small correction factor that barely changes the result
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Display k = 9 × 10⁹ N·m²/C² beside the formula so its size is unmistakable.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: k ≈ 9 × 10⁹ is enormous — that is why charges of only a few nanocoulombs still push measurably.

scene_composition.text: predict: which way do the arrows point?

tts_sentences.text_en: Now flip the second charge to negative. Before I redraw the arrows — predict which way they will point.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब second charge को negative में बदलिए। Arrows को redraw करने से पहले — predict कीजिए कि वे किस direction में point करेंगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు second charge ని negative గా మార్చండి. నేను arrows ని redraw చేయడానికి ముందే — అవి ఏ direction లో point చేస్తాయో predict చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Opposite charges attract, so both arrows now point inward, pulling the charges together.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Opposite charges attract करते हैं, इसलिए अब दोनों arrows अंदर की तरफ point करते हैं, charges को एक-दूसरे की तरफ खींचते हुए।
tts_sentences.text_te: Opposite charges attract అవుతాయి, కాబట్టి ఇప్పుడు రెండు arrows లోపలికి point చేస్తున్నాయి, charges ని ఒకదానివైపు మరొకటి లాగుతున్నాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Like charges repel, unlike charges attract — the sign of the charges sets the direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Like charges repel करते हैं, unlike charges attract करते हैं — charges का sign ही direction तय करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Like charges repel అవుతాయి, unlike charges attract అవుతాయి — charges యొక్క sign మాత్రమే direction ని నిర్ణయిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: But notice the size of the force has not changed at all; only the direction flipped.
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन ध्यान दीजिए, force का size बिल्कुल नहीं बदला; सिर्फ direction पलट गई।
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ గమనించండి, force యొక్క size అస్సలు మారలేదు; కేవలం direction మాత్రమే తిరిగింది.

misconception_watch.belief: The direction of the force is set by the bigger charge, or a negative charge feels no force
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Flip q₂ negative and show BOTH arrows reverse to point inward, still equal in length.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Sign decides direction only: like repel, unlike attract — and a negative charge feels exactly the same size of force.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now make the second charge far bigger than the first — five times the size.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब दूसरे charge को पहले से बहुत बड़ा बनाइए — पाँच गुना बड़ा।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు రెండవ charge ని మొదటి దాని కంటే చాలా పెద్దగా చేయండి — అయిదు రెట్లు పెద్దది.

tts_sentences.text_en: You might expect the big charge to push harder than the little one feels. Watch the arrows.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आप सोच सकते हैं कि बड़ा charge ज़्यादा ज़ोर से push करेगा और छोटा वाला कम महसूस करेगा। arrows को देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీరు అనుకోవచ్చు, పెద్ద charge ఎక్కువగా push చేస్తుందని, చిన్నది తక్కువగా అనుభవిస్తుందని. arrows ని గమనించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: They are exactly the same length. The force on charge one equals the force on charge two.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ये बिल्कुल एक जैसी लंबाई के हैं। force on charge one equals the force on charge two।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇవి సరిగ్గా ఒకే పొడవు కలిగి ఉన్నాయి. force on charge one equals the force on charge two.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is Newton's third law, and the formula shows why: the product q₁ times q₂ is the same for both.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही Newton's third law है, और formula भी यही बताता है: product q₁ times q₂ दोनों के लिए एक ही है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇదే Newton's third law, మరియు formula కూడా అదే చెప్తుంది: product q₁ times q₂ రెండింటికీ ఒకే విధంగా ఉంటుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The bigger or stronger charge pushes harder than the smaller charge feels
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Draw q₂ five times the size of q₁, yet keep both force arrows identical in length.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The pair force is always equal and opposite — q₁q₂ is shared, so neither charge feels more than the other.

tts_sentences.text_en: Keep the charges the same and just move them apart. If I double the distance, what happens to the force? Predict first.
tts_sentences.text_hi: charges को same रखिए और बस उन्हें दूर कीजिए। अगर मैं distance को double करूँ, तो force का क्या होगा? पहले predict कीजिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: charges ని same గా ఉంచి వాటిని దూరంగా జరపండి. నేను distance ని double చేస్తే, force కి ఏమి జరుగుతుంది? ముందు predict చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: It does not halve — it drops to a quarter. The arrow shrinks to one fourth of its length.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह आधी नहीं होती — यह एक चौथाई हो जाती है। arrow सिकुड़कर अपनी लंबाई का one fourth रह जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది సగానికి తగ్గదు — ఇది quarter కి తగ్గిపోతుంది. arrow తన పొడవులో one fourth కి తగ్గిపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Move out to three times the distance and the force falls to one ninth.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तीन गुना distance पर जाइए और force एक नौवें हिस्से तक गिर जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడు రెట్లు distance కి వెళ్తే force one ninth కి పడిపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is the inverse square law: force goes as one over r squared. Distance matters a great deal.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही inverse square law है: force, one over r squared के अनुसार चलती है। distance का बहुत बड़ा फर्क पड़ता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇదే inverse square law: force, one over r squared గా ఉంటుంది. distance చాలా ముఖ్యమైన పాత్ర పోషిస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: Doubling the distance halves the force (the force falls off as 1/r, linearly)
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Step the charge from r to 2r and show the arrow drop to one QUARTER, not one half.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Force falls as 1/r²: double r gives one quarter, triple r gives one ninth.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now hold the distance fixed and double the first charge instead.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब distance को fixed रखिए और इसके बजाय पहले charge को double कीजिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు distance ని fixed గా ఉంచి, మొదటి charge ని మాత్రం double చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Both arrows double in length — the force is twice as strong on each charge.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों arrows की length double हो जाती है — हर charge पर force दोगुनी strong हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు arrows యొక్క length double అవుతాయి — ప్రతి charge మీద force రెండు రెట్లు strong గా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The force is proportional to the product of the charges, q₁ times q₂.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Force, charges के product के proportional होती है, यानी q₁ times q₂।
tts_sentences.text_te: Force అనేది charges యొక్క product కి proportional గా ఉంటుంది, అంటే q₁ times q₂.

tts_sentences.text_en: Double either charge and you double the force; the distance has done nothing here.
tts_sentences.text_hi: किसी भी एक charge को double करें तो force double हो जाती है; यहाँ distance ने कुछ नहीं किया।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఏదైనా ఒక charge ని double చేస్తే force కూడా double అవుతుంది; ఇక్కడ distance ఏమీ చేయలేదు.

misconception_watch.belief: Doubling one charge changes the two arrows by different amounts, or also changes the effect of distance
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Double q₁ at fixed r and show BOTH arrows grow to exactly double, together.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: F ∝ q₁q₂: doubling either charge doubles the force equally on both, with r untouched.

tts_sentences.text_en: So far we have talked about how big the force is. But force is a vector — it has a direction too.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब तक हमने बात की कि force कितनी बड़ी है। लेकिन force एक vector है — इसकी एक direction भी होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పటివరకు మనం force ఎంత పెద్దదో మాట్లాడాము. కానీ force ఒక vector — దానికి ఒక direction కూడా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The Coulomb force always lies along the straight line joining the two charges.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Coulomb force हमेशा उस straight line के along होती है जो दोनों charges को जोड़ती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Coulomb force ఎప్పుడూ రెండు charges ని కలిపే straight line వెంట ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: We write that direction as r-hat, the unit vector pointing from one charge to the other.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हम उस direction को r-hat के रूप में लिखते हैं, जो एक charge से दूसरे charge की तरफ point करने वाला unit vector है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మనం ఆ direction ని r-hat గా రాస్తాము, ఇది ఒక charge నుండి మరొక charge వైపు point చేసే unit vector.

tts_sentences.text_en: Writing it as a vector matters, because it lets us add forces from several charges in the next step.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसे vector के रूप में लिखना ज़रूरी है, क्योंकि इससे हम अगले step में कई charges की forces को add कर सकते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: దీన్ని vector గా రాయడం చాలా ముఖ్యం, ఎందుకంటే దీని వల్ల మనం తర్వాతి step లో అనేక charges నుండి వచ్చే forces ని add చేయగలము.

misconception_watch.belief: The Coulomb force is just a number (a magnitude), with no direction to track
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Show the force arrow pinned along the line joining the charges, labelled with the unit vector r̂.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Force is a vector: it has both a size, k q₁q₂/r², and a direction, along the line joining the charges.

tts_sentences.text_en: Real problems have more than two charges. Here a charge feels a pull from two others at once.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Real problems में दो से ज़्यादा charges होते हैं। यहाँ एक charge एक साथ दो दूसरों से pull feel करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Real problems లో రెండు కంటే ఎక్కువ charges ఉంటాయి. ఇక్కడ ఒక charge ఒకేసారి మరో రెండు charges నుండి pull feel చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Each pair acts on its own: charge one gives force F-one, charge two gives force F-two.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर pair अपने आप act करता है: charge one देता है force F-one, charge two देता है force F-two।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి pair తనంతట తాను act చేస్తుంది: charge one ఇచ్చేది force F-one, charge two ఇచ్చేది force F-two.

tts_sentences.text_en: The net force is not their numbers added — it is the two arrows added tip to tail, as vectors.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Net force उनके numbers को जोड़ने से नहीं मिलती — यह तो वो दो arrows हैं जो tip to tail, vectors की तरह जुड़ते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Net force వాటి numbers కలపడం వల్ల రాదు — అది ఆ రెండు arrows ని tip to tail గా, vectors లా కలిపినప్పుడు వస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Add every pair force as a vector and you get the total. This is the principle of superposition.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर pair की force को vector की तरह जोड़िए और आपको total मिल जाएगी। यही principle of superposition है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి pair force ని vector గా కలిపితే మీకు total వస్తుంది. ఇదే principle of superposition.

misconception_watch.belief: The net force comes only from the nearest or biggest charge, or you just add the force magnitudes
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Show F₁ and F₂ added tip-to-tail into a resultant that points in a new direction, not along either one.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Superposition: add each pair force as a VECTOR (tip to tail), not as plain numbers, and every charge counts.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive it. Slide the two charges and the distance and watch the force respond live.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आप खुद चलाइए। दोनों charges और distance को slide कीजिए और देखिए कैसे force live respond करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరే నడపండి. రెండు charges మరియు distance ని slide చేయండి మరియు force ఎలా live గా respond చేస్తుందో చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Raise either charge and the arrows grow together; the force tracks the product q₁ times q₂.
tts_sentences.text_hi: किसी भी charge को बढ़ाइए और arrows एक साथ बड़े होते हैं; force, product q₁ times q₂ को track करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఏదైనా charge ని పెంచండి మరియు arrows కలిసి పెద్దవి అవుతాయి; force అనేది product q₁ times q₂ ని track చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Push the charges apart and feel how fast the force fades — that is the inverse square at work.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Charges को दूर कीजिए और महसूस कीजिए कि force कितनी तेज़ी से fade होती है — यही inverse square का कमाल है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Charges ని దూరంగా నెట్టండి మరియు force ఎంత వేగంగా fade అవుతుందో అనుభవించండి — అదే inverse square పని చేయడం.

tts_sentences.text_en: Flip a charge negative and the arrows turn inward to attract. One law, every case.
tts_sentences.text_hi: किसी charge को negative कर दीजिए और arrows inward मुड़ जाते हैं, attract करने लगते हैं। एक ही law, हर case में।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక charge ని negative గా flip చేయండి మరియు arrows inward గా తిరిగి attract చేస్తాయి. ఒకే law, ప్రతి case లో.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered Coulomb's law when they can compute the force between two point charges from F = k q₁q₂/r² (handling the nanocoulomb and centimetre units), predict attraction versus repulsion from the signs alone, apply the equal-and-opposite pair force (Newton's third law) no matter how unequal the charges are, reason numerically that the force falls as 1/r² (double r → one quarter) and grows as the product q₁q₂ (double a charge → double the force), and find the net force on a charge from several others as the VECTOR sum of the separate pair forces (superposition). The dielectric-medium factor F = F_vacuum/K is taught in a separate simulation and is not assessed here.

questions.stem: Two charges of +5 nC each are placed 5 cm apart in vacuum. What is the force between them, and in which direction does each charge feel it?

options.A: About 9 × 10⁻⁵ N, directed outward — each charge is pushed away from the other (repulsion)
options.B: About 9 × 10⁻⁵ N, directed inward — the two charges are pulled together (attraction)
options.C: Zero, because the two charges are equal and therefore cancel each other out
options.D: About 45 N, found by multiplying the charges and dividing by the distance without squaring it

questions.tested_idea: Apply F = k q₁q₂/r² with correct nC and cm units; like charges repel, so each is pushed outward.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two +3 nC charges sit 6 cm apart in vacuum. Estimate the force on each charge and state which way it acts.

questions.stem: Charge A is +10 nC and charge B is +1 nC, held a fixed distance apart. How does the force ON A compare with the force ON B?

options.A: They are equal in size and opposite in direction — A feels exactly the same force B does
options.B: The force on A is 10 times larger, because A carries ten times the charge
options.C: The force on B is 10 times larger, because the bigger charge A pushes the smaller one harder
options.D: It cannot be compared without also knowing the masses of the two charges

questions.tested_idea: The pair force is equal and opposite (Newton's third law); the shared product q₁q₂ means neither charge feels more, however unequal they are.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A +20 nC charge and a +2 nC charge are a set distance apart. Compare the magnitude of the electric force each one experiences.

questions.stem: Two charges feel a force F. Without changing the charges, you double the distance between them. What is the new force?

options.A: F/4 — the force falls as the inverse square of the distance
options.B: F/2 — doubling the distance halves the force
options.C: 2F — moving the charges apart increases the force
options.D: F — the distance does not affect the strength of the force

questions.tested_idea: F ∝ 1/r²: doubling the separation cuts the force to one quarter, not one half.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two charges experience a force F. If you move them three times as far apart, what does the force become?

questions.stem: Two charges feel a force F. Keeping the distance the same, you triple ONE of the charges. What is the new force?

options.A: 3F — the force is proportional to the product of the charges
options.B: 9F — tripling a charge multiplies the force by three squared
options.C: F/3 — increasing a charge reduces the force on it
options.D: F — only the distance, not the charges, changes the force

questions.tested_idea: F ∝ q₁q₂: tripling one charge triples the force (the square only applies to distance, not charge).
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two charges feel a force F. If you double one of the two charges while holding the separation fixed, what is the new force?

questions.stem: A +q and a −q attract each other with force F. You now make BOTH charges negative, keeping their sizes and separation the same. What happens to the force?

options.A: Same size F, but now repulsive — the magnitude k q₁q₂/r² is unchanged; only the direction reverses
options.B: The force vanishes, because two negative charges cancel each other
options.C: The force is still attractive, because the charges have the same size
options.D: The force becomes half as strong, because both charges are now the same sign

questions.tested_idea: Sign sets direction only: two like charges repel, but the magnitude k q₁q₂/r² is the same as before.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two opposite charges attract with force F. If you flip both signs so they are now both positive, what is the new force?

questions.stem: A charge sits between two others and feels a force F₁ pointing left from one and an equal force F₂ pointing straight up from the other. What is the net force on it?

options.A: The vector sum of F₁ and F₂ — pointing diagonally up-and-left, with magnitude √(F₁² + F₂²)
options.B: F₁ + F₂ as plain numbers, pointing left because the left force comes first
options.C: Only F₁, because the net force is set by the single nearest or strongest charge
options.D: Zero, because a leftward force and an upward force always cancel

questions.tested_idea: Superposition: the net force is the VECTOR sum of the separate pair forces, not a scalar sum and not just the nearest charge.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A charge is pulled by one neighbour with a force pointing right and by another with an equal force pointing down. What is the resultant force on it?

STATE_1.label: Two charges push on each other
